API Usage

2026-04-20

Source: vignettes/api.Rmd
api.Rmd

Storr setup

Create a storr either via storr_tiledb():

uri <- tempfile()
sto <- storr_tiledb(uri, init = TRUE)

Or more explicitly:

uri2 <- tempfile()
dr <- driver_tiledb(uri2, init = TRUE)
sto2 <- storr::storr(dr)

The first approach generates a TileDBStorr object that represents the storr interface optimised for TileDB storage and provides additional functionality. The latter is the standard storr object.

API Usage

1. get, set, del 

dat <- head(mtcars, 2)

# set
sto$set("a", dat)
sto$set("b", list(name = "John"))
sto$mset(c("a", "b", "c"), value = list(dat, dat, dat), namespace = "ns1")

# get
sto$get("b")
# $name
# [1] "John"
sto$mget(c("b", "b"), namespace = c("objects", "ns1"))
# [[1]]
# [[1]]$name
# [1] "John"
# 
# 
# [[2]]
#               mpg cyl disp  hp drat    wt  qsec vs am gear carb
# Mazda RX4      21   6  160 110  3.9 2.620 16.46  0  1    4    4
# Mazda RX4 Wag  21   6  160 110  3.9 2.875 17.02  0  1    4    4

# list keys
sto$list("objects")
# [1] "a" "b"
sto$list("ns1")
# [1] "a" "b" "c"

# list namespaces
sto$list_namespaces()
# [1] "ns1"     "objects"

# list hashes
sto$list_hashes()
# [1] "c184c6034d956360b5bb682fcd4b6cb8" "fd441562b6f1ec33e42a4369820cb0ae"

# del
sto$del("a")
sto$del("b")
sto$exists("a")
# [1] FALSE

# Retrieve hashes and namespaces
sto$list_namespaces()
# [1] "ns1"
sto$list_hashes()
# [1] "c184c6034d956360b5bb682fcd4b6cb8" "fd441562b6f1ec33e42a4369820cb0ae"

# Delete unused hash
sto$gc()
sto$list_hashes()
# [1] "c184c6034d956360b5bb682fcd4b6cb8"

# Delete by namespace
sto$clear("ns1")
# [1] TRUE
sto$list("ns1")
# character(0)

2. get_keymeta, set_keymeta

With a key-namespace pair, you can attached notes and expiration metadata.

Set key metadata when creating a key:

# Expires in 5 minutes
sto$set("aa", 1, expires_at = Sys.time() + 60 * 5, 
        notes = "{\"name\":\"John\",\"id\":12345}")

# Expires in 1 second
sto$set("bb", 1, expires_at = Sys.time() + 1)

Or update/retrieve with $set_keymeta() and $get_keymeta() methods:

# Retrieve key metadata
sto$get_keymeta("aa", use_cache = FALSE)
sto$mget_keymeta(c("aa", "bb"), use_cache = TRUE)

# Update key metadata
sto$set_keymeta("bb", notes = "Updated Note")
sto$get_keymeta("bb")

Expiration management

# Retrieve keys with expiration
sto$keys_with_expiration()
#    namespace    key          expires_at
#       <char> <char>              <POSc>
# 1:   objects     aa 2026-04-20 10:55:26
# 2:   objects     bb 2026-04-20 10:50:27

Sys.sleep(1)

# Retrieve expired keys
sto$expired_keys()
#    namespace    key          expires_at
#       <char> <char>              <POSc>
# 1:   objects     bb 2026-04-20 10:50:27

# Remove expired keys
sto$clear_expired_keys()

# Check for expired keys
sto$has_expired_keys()
# [1] FALSE

# Or via gc()
sto$gc(clear_expired = TRUE)

Clear metadata

sto$clr_keymeta("aa")
sto$get_keymeta("aa")
# $expires_at
# [1] NA
# 
# $notes
# [1] NA

3. Async methods

Set keys without blocking R session:

Sys.sleep(1)
sto$set_async("abc", 1)
sto$set_async("abc2", 2)

4. Fragments Management

When writing data to TileDB, it generates multiple array fragments on disk. Over time, these files need to be consolidated in order to improve query performance by reducing I/O overhead.

Note that the consolidation operation does not delete the old fragments; it only creates new consolidated fragments. The actual cleanup of old fragments is performed by the separate vacuum operation.

To manage storr TileDB fragments use storr_fragments() which encapsulates the consolidation and vacuum operations.

# Set up a new storr
uri <- tempfile()
sto <- storr_tiledb(uri, init = TRUE)

# Set 3 keys and 2 objects
sto$set("a", 1)
sto$set("b", 2)
sto$set("c", 1)
# Initialise StorrFragments class
fosto <- storr_fragments(uri)

# Total fragments (5, 3 for keys, 2 for hashes)
fosto$frag_num()
# [1] 5

# Total fragments to vacuum
fosto$to_vacuum_num()
# [1] 0

Consolidate everything (both arrays)

# Consolidate all
fosto$consolidate()
# [1] TRUE
# Total fragments (2, 1 for keys, 1 for hashes)
fosto$frag_num()
# [1] 2

# Total fragments to vacuum (5)
fosto$to_vacuum_num()
# [1] 5

Now, let’s clean up the old fragments.

# Cleaup old fragments
fosto$vacuum()
# [1] TRUE

# Total fragments to vacuum (0)
fosto$to_vacuum_num()
# [1] 0

On this page
